title: cerasus vulgaris var. frutescens
intro: Cerasus vulgaris var. frutescens, also known as the European wild cherry or forest cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Cerasus vulgaris var. frutescens include its ability to relieve fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and other medicinal products.
